data.acceptance:
1. `translatePart` in src/lib/opencode-http-session.ts (or an extracted helper module) emits Claude-shape JSON for OpenCode parts of type `tool` (with `state.input`/`state.output`/`state.status`), `reasoning` (text), `file`, `snapshot`, and `error`, in addition to today's `text`/`step-start`/`step-finish`.
2. `translateEvent` in the same module forwards `message.updated`, `message.part.updated`, `step.updated`, `session.idle`, `session.error` (in addition to today's permission events), preserving the originating event payload so downstream renderers can use it.
3. `createOpenCodeNormalizer` in src/lib/agent-adapter-normalizers.ts produces Claude-shape `assistant` blocks with `tool_use` content for tool calls, `user` blocks with `tool_result` content for tool outputs, `stream_event` deltas for reasoning, and `result` with `is_error: true` for `session.error`/error step finishes.
4. A new `formatOpenCodeEvent` (in a new `src/lib/opencode-event-format.ts`) is wired into `formatStreamEvent` in src/lib/terminal-manager-format.ts and renders tool calls (▶ <tool> <summary>), tool results (dim, clipped to ~1500 chars with status tag when non-success), reasoning (magenta), step boundaries, and session idle/error markers.
5. Hermetic unit tests cover translatePart, translateEvent, the normalizer additions, and formatOpenCodeEvent. All tests run under `bun run test` without touching network/fs/env.
6. End-to-end manual run: starting `bun run dev` in the worktree and dispatching a take to OpenCode shows tool calls, tool outputs, and reasoning in the terminal pane (evidence captured in the knot's SHIPPING note).
7. `bun run lint`, `bunx tsc --noEmit`, `bun run test`, and `bun run build` all complete with zero new failures relative to `origin/main`.
